hub.title: Fantasy MX guides and rules
hub.subtitle: Learn how to play every mode in the app: fantasy with a live draft, exact-score pools and Survivor with lives. Available for Liga MX, Premier League, LaLiga, Serie A and Bundesliga.

nav.ctaText: Create your free account and put what you learned into practice.

fantasy.title: How to play Fantasy on Fantasy MX
fantasy.excerpt: Create your league, run the draft, set your lineup and win your weekly head-to-head matchups.
fantasy.intro: Fantasy mode turns every matchday into a competition between you and your friends. You build a team with real players from the league of your choice — Liga MX, Premier League, LaLiga, Serie A or Bundesliga — and every week you face another team from your league head to head. Whoever scores more points with their starters wins.

sections.p: A fantasy league is a group where each participant manages their own team. Your real players score for you: if your striker scores, you earn points; if your keeper keeps a clean sheet, you do too. What makes Fantasy MX different is that you don't just pile up points in a table: every matchday you play a direct matchup against another participant, just like a real league.
When you create a league you choose its name, the competition it runs on and the number of participants; you become the admin and define the scoring rules. To join a league there are two ways in:

list.d: The admin shares an access code or an invite link so you can enter their private league.
From the Discover tab you can find open leagues by name and join directly.

sections.p: Before the draft you give your team an identity: a name, initials and, if you want, your own crest. That's the team your rivals will see every matchday in the matchups.

Every league starts with a live draft: taking turns against the clock, each participant picks players until their squad is complete. Each player can only belong to one team within the league. We have a guide dedicated to the draft.

sections.h: Your squad: starters, FLEX and bench
sections.p: Your squad is organized by position according to the formation your league defines. Only starters score points in the matchup:

list.d: The fixed spots in your eleven: goalkeeper, defenders, midfielders and forwards, in the numbers your league's formation requires.
A wildcard spot you can fill with players from different positions. It gives you room to start whoever is in form.
Your substitutes. They don't score points, but you can swap them with your starters before each matchday.

sections.note: Check injuries, suspensions and the schedule before the matchday kicks off: bench points don't count toward the score.

sections.p: Every matchday the app schedules home-and-away duels between the teams in your league. The matchup score is the sum of each team's starters' points: whoever scores more wins; if you finish level, it's a draw. The result feeds the league table with wins, draws and losses, points for and against, just like a real football table.

sections.h: Strengthen your team during the season
sections.p: Your squad isn't final. Throughout the season you can add available players nobody has picked and propose trades to adjust your team matchday by matchday. Position, team and stats filters help you find reinforcements.
Matchday after matchday, your results accumulate in your league's standings. Whoever drafts best, sets the best lineups and strengthens their team best finishes on top.

draft.title: How the live draft works
draft.excerpt: Real-time turns, a pick timer and auto-pick so the draft never stalls.
draft.intro: The draft is the moment when players get distributed among all the participants of a fantasy league. On Fantasy MX it's a live event: everyone connected at once, picking in turns, with a timer for every selection and instant updates.

sections.p: The draft is a turn-based selection. Each player in the competition can only belong to one team within your league, so once someone picks them, they're gone for everyone else. That's why every turn counts.

The league admin is the one who activates the draft when the group is ready. At that moment every participant gets a notification to enter the room. Until then, the app shows you whether the draft is pending, active or completed.
The room shows the pick order and whose turn it is at every moment. Each pick has a time limit configured by the league:

list.d: The app alerts you when you're up and the clock starts ticking. Pick before it hits zero.
If your league has it enabled and your time runs out, the app picks for you so the draft never stalls. It also helps when you know you won't be able to connect.

sections.p: The room shows who's online in real time, and the draft board records every selection round by round. That way you know which positions your rivals have covered and which gaps they still have.
Come prepared and never depend on a single name:

list.d: Have several targets per position: someone will very likely take your favorite before your turn.
Remember your lineup needs a goalkeeper, defenders, midfielders, forwards and a FLEX. Don't overload one position.
In the search you can filter by position and team, and compare total points, average and games played.
The last rounds are for substitutes who save you on matchdays with absences.

quiniela.title: How to play the pool
quiniela.excerpt: Predict the exact score of every match and compete with your group matchday after matchday.
quiniela.intro: The pool is the fastest, most social mode on Fantasy MX. Every matchday you predict the exact score of the matches and compete with your friends to see who gets the most right. No draft, no team management: just your football knowledge.

sections.p: A pool is a private group where everyone predicts the results of a specific league and stage — for example, the current Liga MX tournament or the Premier League season. Every correct call earns points and the group table updates as matches are played.

You can create your own pool by choosing the league and stage it will run on, and invite your people with an access code or a shareable link. Groups are private: you only compete with the people you invite.

For each match you enter an exact score: goals for the home side and goals for the away side. You can adjust your prediction as many times as you like until the match kicks off; once the ball rolls, it locks. There's only one prediction per match: if you change it before kickoff, it simply replaces the previous one.
Each group defines how many points every type of correct call is worth, always across three tiers:

list.d: You nailed the final score exactly. It's the best-paid call.
You missed the score but called who would win the match.
You predicted a draw and the match ended level, even if with a different score.

sections.note: Points don't stack: if you hit the exact score, you earn only that tier's points. Check your group's scoring rules in the info tab.

sections.p: As matches are played, your pool's standings update live. You can view each matchday's table or the overall tournament total, and show off your calls to your group.

survivor.title: How Survivor works
survivor.excerpt: Pick one winner per matchday without repeating teams. Manage your lives and be the last one standing.
survivor.intro: Survivor is Fantasy MX's elimination mode. Every matchday you pick one team you think will win. If it wins, you stay alive; if it loses, you lose a life. When you run out of lives, you're out. The last one standing wins.

sections.p: Every matchday you pick a single team from the available matches. Your pick lives or dies with that match's result, so choose wisely.
Each participant starts with the number of lives the pool defines (usually one). Here's what happens to your pick every matchday:

list.d: You survive and advance to the next matchday with your lives intact.
A draw costs no life: you survive, though the team is used up.
Missing the deadline also costs a life. Don't let the matchday slip by.

sections.note: When you reach zero lives you're eliminated from the Survivor. If the pool hands out more than one life, a stumble won't send you home, but every mistake counts.

sections.p: Once you use a team, it's burned for the rest of the tournament: you can't pick it again in later matchdays. That's the real challenge of Survivor — deciding when to spend the favorites.
You can change your pick as many times as you like while the matchday hasn't started. As soon as the first match of the matchday kicks off, it locks completely: no more picking or changing.

Picking the week's favorite isn't enough. Since you can't repeat, look at the schedule for upcoming matchdays and save certain teams for the tough dates. In My Picks you have your full history of wins, draws and losses to plan what's next.
The Survivor ends when the tournament runs out of matchdays or when a single survivor remains, depending on how the pool is configured. Outlast everyone and the glory is yours.

puntos.title: The fantasy scoring system
puntos.excerpt: How goals, assists and real performances become points, and how they decide your matchups.
puntos.intro: In Fantasy mode, your players score based on what they actually do on the pitch. Those points decide your matchups every matchday. This guide explains how they're calculated and why they can change from league to league.

sections.p: After every match, Fantasy MX takes your players' real stats — goals, assists, minutes, clean sheets, cards and more — and turns them into points for your team. It works the same whether your league runs on Liga MX, the Premier League, LaLiga, Serie A or the Bundesliga.
Positive actions add points, like a goal or an assist, and some negative actions subtract, like a red card. The idea is to reward the players who truly influence the match.

The same action can be worth different amounts depending on the player's position. For example, a goal usually pays more for a defender than for a forward, because it's less common. That balances the value of every position in your lineup.

Each fantasy league's admin configures the scoring rules: which actions score, how many points they're worth per position and under what conditions. Two leagues can reward different things, so check your league's rules before building your team.

Your weekly matchup score is the sum of your starters' points, including the FLEX spot. Your bench players accumulate stats, but their points don't count toward the duel: setting a good lineup matters as much as drafting well.

In every matchup you can see the breakdown of how much each of your players scored, and the player search shows their total points, average and games played. It's the best way to understand what worked and adjust your lineup for the next matchday.
